commitMessage:
Bug 576448 - Compile error shown when working with records though
everything runs fine

Change-Id: I6861c6ebab950fe696a9b766cd176809d1ca2bd8
Signed-off-by: Manoj Palat <manpalat@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/+/186521
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: The change is a specific internal refactoring within Eclipse JDT's DOM API to simplify bitwise flag checking with a dedicated method. It is not a general Java language pattern or common API modernization for application code, warranting a new category for JDT internal specifics.
languageChangeNeeded: Support for bitwise operators (`&`) in pattern matching and replacements is required.
summary: This commit simplifies an internal JDT (Java Development Tools) `MethodBinding` implementation by replacing a bitwise check on `tagBits` (`((this.binding.tagBits & TagBits.IsCanonicalConstructor) != 0)`) with a dedicated method call (`this.binding.isCanonicalConstructor()`). This improves internal code clarity and relies on a more robust API. However, the TriggerPattern DSL currently does not support bitwise operators (`&`) in patterns, making this transformation impossible to implement with the existing DSL. Therefore, it is classified as RED. The context of the bug (working with records) implies a Java 17+ environment where record support is finalized.

commitMessage:
Bug 576948 - [18] Create Java 18 version and supporting code in tests

Change-Id: Ic485c927b21f4e5ab730ed9a87aa7c069a284830
Signed-off-by: Jay Arthanareeswaran <jarthana@in.ibm.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/+/187571
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
categoryReason: The change is a specific refactoring within the Eclipse JDT Core DOM API, related to managing Java Language Specification versions, which aligns with existing 'JDT Internal Refactoring' patterns.
summary: This commit introduces support for Java Language Specification 18 (JLS18) within the Eclipse JDT Core DOM API. The most direct and generalizable pattern for a DSL rule is the mechanical replacement of calls to the internal `supportedOnlyIn17()` method with `supportedOnlyIn18()`. This is a straightforward method call replacement that directly reflects the API modernization for handling JLS version checks. Other changes, such as adding constants (`JLS18`) or updating field initializers (`JLS_INTERNAL_Latest = JLS17` to `JLS18`), are not directly expressible as DSL expression transformations.

irrelevantReason: This commit updates the internal definition of the `org.eclipse.jdt.core.dom.AST.JLS_Latest` constant from `JLS17` to `JLS18` within the JDT Core library. It is an API evolution change and not a pattern of code usage that can be generalized into a reusable transformation rule for end-users. The constant `JLS_Latest` itself is already deprecated in favor of `getJLSLatest()`, and rules for migrating specific JLS versions (like JLS16) to `JLS_Latest` have been previously identified. This commit modifies the target of `JLS_Latest`, not its usage.
summary: This commit is an internal API update in JDT Core, changing the definition of `AST.JLS_Latest` to point to `JLS18`. It is not a user-facing code transformation pattern and therefore not relevant for DSL rule mining.

commitMessage:

Change-Id: I5cfc5d565963330d44864d1fd37b863b7fd73a97

Reviewed-by: Vikas Chandra <Vikas.Chandra@in.ibm.com>
categoryReason: This commit involves internal changes to the Eclipse JDT DOM (Document Object Model) parser to enhance its Javadoc processing capabilities.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL currently does not support changing method signatures (especially generic type parameters) or injecting complex multi-line conditional logic (if/else if blocks with type checks and casts) as part of a replacement. The replacement mechanism is primarily for expressions or simple multiline sequences of statements, not for creating new control flow structures or modifying method declarations.
summary: This commit modifies an internal JDT DOM parser method (`DocCommentParser.addTagProperties`) to allow `TagProperty` values to be either `String` or `ASTNode`, enhancing Javadoc `@snippet` support. The changes involve modifying a method signature from `Map<String, String>` to `Map<String, Object>` and introducing a conditional `if-else if` block within a lambda to handle different value types. These types of structural changes, especially modifying method signatures and inserting complex control flow into a replacement, are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL. Therefore, it is classified as RED.

categoryReason: The change concerns the internal API of Eclipse JDT Core DOM, specifically how project context is handled during parsing.
languageChangeNeeded:
The DSL would need to support: 
1. Matching and refactoring method signatures (changing parameter types).
2. Removing local variable declarations and replacing their usages with other expressions or fields (requiring more advanced data-flow analysis).
3. Recognizing and acting on specific 'this.field' references, which currently might not be universally patternable when the target expression is a field rather than a placeholder binding.
summary: This commit refactors internal JDT Core DOM APIs (`ASTParser`, `CompilationUnitResolver`) to pass an `IJavaProject` object directly instead of a derived `String` project path. This supports new Javadoc `@snippet` properties by providing richer project context. The changes involve modifying method signatures, removing a local variable, and updating call sites to pass `this.project`. These structural changes, particularly method signature modifications and the removal/replacement of a local variable with a field, are beyond the current capabilities of the TriggerPattern DSL, which focuses on expression-level and annotation-level rewrites.
